page: Microsoft Azure E4s_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the Esv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 2 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8573C processor operating at 4.2 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The processor is equipped with a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache, alongside advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, Intel AMX, and AES cryptography acceleration. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, relying instead on Premium Storage capabilities for external disk attachments.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities but moderate multi-core scaling. Geekbench single-core testing scored 2146.0, and single-threaded gzip compression reached 31.25 GB/sec. However, multi-core stress-ng CPU performance and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression fell into the weaker tier, reflecting the physical core limit of two cores with hyper-threading. Memory bandwidth tests showed a weak read-only large block rate of 36.52 GB/sec, though write bandwidth remained average at 25.66 GB/sec. In database and application workloads, the instance achieved average scores in Passmark database operations and static web serving throughput, while Redis SET operations registered a weaker 5.69 million operations per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E4s_v6 offers a dense memory allocation relative to its compute footprint, making it a viable option for workloads that require substantial RAM without demanding massive parallel processing power. The primary tradeoff is the limited multi-core performance and lower memory read bandwidth under large block sizes. This hardware profile is best suited for memory-intensive workloads such as relational databases, caching layers, moderate web serving, and lightweight text or image processing tasks. It can also handle small-scale language model inference, though text generation speeds drop significantly on larger models.
description: Microsoft Azure E4s_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Esv6 family, powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 instance features a large 260 MB L3 cache and advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. It lacks local storage and integrated GPUs, relying on Premium Storage for disk operations. Benchmarks reveal strong single-core performance, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 2146.0 and strong single-threaded gzip compression, though multi-core and memory read bandwidth metrics are weaker. Qualitatively, the E4s_v6 provides high memory density relative to its compute footprint, making it cost-effective for memory-bound applications. It is well-suited for relational databases, caching layers, static web serving, and lightweight text processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E4s_v6 is a memory-optimized instance with 4 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8573C CPU, offering strong single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E4s_v6 is a memory-optimized VM featuring 4 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, designed for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high RAM-to-vCPU density and strong single-core Intel Xeon performance for memory-bound workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 4.2 GHz clock speed
32.0 GB of RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per core memory-to-vCPU ratio
Strong single-core performance in Geekbench and single-threaded compression
No local storage or bundled GPUs, utilizing Premium Storage for disk needs
Best suited for relational databases, caching layers, and static web serving
